drop_reason_overturned: Prior DROP cited a 737-byte canonical_patch containing only a scratch reproducer file (keybind-conflict-scope-test.ts). That claim is technically true of the canonical_patch JSON — but the substantive edits to packages/coding-agent/src/core/keybindings.ts (op[17]), packages/coding-agent/src/core/extensions/runner.ts (op[49]), and packages/coding-agent/CHANGELOG.md (op[62]) are flagged in _reconstruction_warnings as 'edit input has no old/new text (export likely stripped content)'. This is the documented hashline-format pi-mono export artifact, NOT actual missing session work. Empirical agent work in patch_library/ confirms: opus46 (25 ops), deepseek_v4_pro (25 ops), deepseek_v4_flash (29 ops), minimax25 (21 ops), minimax27 (35 ops) all touched the canonical files runner.ts and keybindings.ts (both coding-agent and tui packages). The session is real, the fix is well-defined, and the buggy base commit (8a8e2a8049fba194d999cf7f3ec4b247910657ee) is properly pinned in environment/Dockerfile.

rescue_evidence.legacy_verifier_design: 5 weighted F2P gates (sum=1.00) targeting scope-aware keybinding conflict detection: t6_f2p_keybindings_have_scope_tags (0.30), t6_f2p_runner_consults_scope (0.20), t11_f2p_picker_scope_keys_count (0.20), t11_f2p_global_editor_scope_keys_present (0.15), t13_f2p_runner_tests_pass (0.15). Gates inspect runner.ts and keybindings.ts for scope: tags + scope-aware logic + behavioral bun test execution. Plus 1 P2P_GATING (file-existence) + 4 P2P_REGRESSION (informational tsgo/vitest checks).
rescue_evidence.fix_summary_calibration: nop_baseline=0.05, gold_fix_reward=1.00, multi-turn minimax-m2=0.60 (partial). Documented spread > 0.6 — task discriminates.
rescue_evidence.current_test_sh_status: swerebench-style with empty FAIL_TO_PASS=[] and PASS_TO_PASS=[] in install_config.json — falls back to overall vitest pass rate, which doesn't measure the scope-tagging behavior the session targets. Legacy verifier is the correct scoring shape and is present as a .bak; rescue should restore it.
rescue_evidence.user_simulation_prompt_quality: 26 verbatim user turns with specific observable trigger conditions; documents key state transitions (T6=first implementation, T11=scope-aware warnings work, T13=full behavior). Calibrated.
rescue_evidence.session_resolution: stuck (commit-message bikeshed in turns 24-26; the technical fix in turns 6-15 was completed cleanly)

stripped_due_to: hashline-format pi-mono session export (tool_replay reconstruction) does not preserve oldText/newText for Edit ops; warnings explicitly documented in canonical_patch._reconstruction_warnings
rescue_action: Restore tests/test.sh from tests/test.sh.legacy.bak and tests/test_manifest.yaml from tests/test_manifest.yaml.legacy.bak. The current swerebench-style test.sh with empty F2P/P2P arrays measures overall vitest pass rate, which does not score scope-aware behavior. The legacy verifier (5 weighted F2P gates with documented nop=0.05/gold=1.00 spread) is the correct shape for this task and lives in-tree.
notes: Instruction.md is intentionally underspecified ('look at previous sessions and find the session where we looked for handling false positive in keybinds overlap') because it is the verbatim first user turn of a multi-turn correction loop. The user_simulation_prompt.md exists and provides 26 calibrated trigger conditions to drive the agent through the fix. This is the canonical multi-turn rescue pattern: instruction is purposely thin, sim provides ground truth, verifier scores the resulting code. Prior DROP misread the export-pipeline artifact as missing session work and missed the legacy verifier .bak files entirely.
